dayName: First Day at the Crow's Wing

scenarios.title: The Merchant's Lost Wagon
scenarios.target: recover the salt-merchant's wagon from the brigands camped at the Crow's Ford

contributions.line: Marek, muscles tensed, struggled with the heavy iron lock, his brawny arms straining against rusted bolts.
Roselle crept through the treeline, her light-footed steps avoiding every snapped twig and loose stone.

scenarios.outcomeNarrative: Marek's brute force failed to crack the strongbox, and Roselle's careful silence couldn't get past the sentry in time; the brigands remain unaware of the attempted theft.

scenarios.title: The Brigand's Tongue
scenarios.target: wring the location of the bandit cache out of the prisoner before dawn

contributions.line: Marek's muscular frame leans into the threat, voice low and commanding, trying to break through the prisoner's false calm.
Veska watches the lies shift and flickers her gaze, catching the small contradictions in his story, unshaken.

scenarios.outcomeNarrative: Marek's physical intimidation rattled the prisoner just enough, while Veska's sharp eye caught the inconsistencies. Under the combined pressure, the captive finally cracked, revealing the location of the bandit's cache.

scenarios.title: Watchtower at the Pass
scenarios.target: commission a new watchtower at the southern pass before winter closes the road

contributions.line: Veska's cold analysis ensures the design is resilient enough for the spring melt, despite her fatigue.
Marek's brawny oversight keeps the crew honest, but his fatigue shows as he struggles to keep morale up.

scenarios.outcomeNarrative: Veska’s precise calculations help draft a structure that might stand, but Marek’s forceful presence can't rally enough effort to finish it. The split decision leaves the project in a fragile state, vulnerable to the coming thaw.
